ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #37, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Sep-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Bret appeared by zoom for Walter Sullivan.

The Court stated that the only issue is the trustee's last manager fees, and that at the last accounting the manager fees requested were at a rate of $175.00 per hour, but the court approved the fees at $125.00 per hour. Mr. Bret stated that his client is prepared to accept $125.00 per hour and that no other matters were raised by the beneficiaries. The Thirty-Seventh Account and Report of Trustee and Petition for Settlement, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #37, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Aug-31-2021 Continued To Sep-21-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #36, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Bret appeared by zoom for Walter Sullivan (Trustee).

The Court informed Mr. Sullivan that the request for up to 36 hours in Attorney fees for post filing of a petition will not ordinarily be granted and that it should be included in the next accounting. The Court also stated that the Trustee and the LLC Manager fee was at $100.00 per hour in the past three accountings and it has been a long time, and that it would be appropriate to increase it to $125.00 per hour. Mr. Bret stated that $125.00 per hour is acceptable, that there were substantial post-petition documents that he needed to reply to, that there was a 20-page response by a Beneficiary, that there was a 126-page declaration by the Beneficiaries Attorney, that there was 36 to 60-hours of work and that it is a long time to wait for a small firm. The Court informed Mr. Bret that the appropriate way to address this is to file an amendment to the petition, or include it in the next accounting period. Mr. Bret asked the Court to not allow his request. The Thirty-Sixth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Cabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #35, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-11-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Bret for Walter Sullivan, III (Petitioner).
Steven Bovarnick and James Allen (Beneficiary).

Mr. Bovarnick stated that he received the reply, but he did not get the Examiner's notes and that he filed a response because they are seeking information under 16061. Mr. Bret stated that there is no objection to the accounting, that Ms. Escher has indicated that $100.00 per hour for Trustee's fees is okay, that all of the other issues in the response are the same as what's been hashed out for the last three years, and that because there is no objection, he is requesting a ruling on the accounting. Mr. Bovarnick stated that he believes the accounting should be taken under submission, and raised the issue of the Trustee's time sheets and the reasonableness of the Trustee's hours. Mr. Bret stated that for the past 32 years the Trustee's compensation was awarded at the fair market value of the Trust and that only in the last three years Judge Busch has looked at the hours. The Thirty-Fifth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #35, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-12-2019 Continued To Sep-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #34, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Bret for Walter Sullivan (Petitioner/Trustee).
James Allen and Steve Bovarnick for and with Paula Escher.

Mr. Bret stated that Ms. Escher has made no objection to the account itself, that she made comments with respect to the Trustee's hourly rate and that they have conceded to a rate of $100.00 per hour. Mr. Bret also stated that they are in the same position as they were last year, that there is a difference of opinion as to whether the request for documents are reasonable and a difference of opinion as to whether the documents that have been provided are adequate, that the Trustee has prepared accountings timely and that the Trustee has quarterly conference calls. The Court raised the issue of the Trustee wanting to resign. Mr. Bret stated that the Trustee wants to transition, that they had a meeting and a conference, that when his Client is confident that the Successor Trustee is up to date, he will transition out, and that there is no fixed date. Mr. Bovarnick noted that other Family members are here to see what goes on, that his roll is to make sure there is transparency, that there has been improvement and it is getting better, that he is happy to hear the Court is open to the rate of $100.00 per hour being appropriate and that the nature of the work is not necessarily the work of a professional. The Court stated that there is no question there is a whole variety of different tasks and functions in these hours. The Thirty Fourth Account and Report of Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Santiago. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #34, REPORT, FEES

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #34, REPORT, FEES

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #33, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-04-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Steve Bovarnik and Jim Allen for Paula Escher (Beneficiary).
Thomas Bret for Walter Sullivan (Trustee).

The Court expressed concern about how much benefit the Trustee gets from the Trust in comparison to how little gets out to the other Beneficiaries, as well as the amount/size of fees being requested. Mr. Bret stated that Mr. Sullivan acknowledges that he has been tardy in responding, that he has 15 years experience in a Trust Company, that the net income nearly doubled and that the warehouses are rented out. The 33rd Account and Report, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #33, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-03-2017 Continued To Nov-07-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Thirty-Second Account And Report Of Trustee And Request For Approval Of Trustee Fees And Attorneys'
Off Calendar Sep-21-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Bret for (trustee) Walter Sullivan III.

The Court stated that this is the 32nd report and account and that it is seeing only $80,000.00 coming out to the beneficiaries due to fees and administrative costs. Mr. Bret stated that the property is a vineyard and wedding venue site, that it is expensive to maintain, that the trust instructed that the property be maintained, that the last few accountings have been similar, that there is substantial benefit to the trustee's siblings which is not evident in the accounting, that the trustee's declaration explains the benefits, that in the will Mr. Sullivan was the only beneficiary given the right to reside in the property but he has made the property available to his sisters and their children. The Court stated that it understands that there have been no objections. Mr. Bret explained that he is fairly new to the case and that his predecessor Mr. Kolb had drafted the will and knew exactly what Ms. de Pins wanted. The Court stated that the will talks about not selling the property but also talks about what is to happen if it is sold. Mr. Bret read instructions regarding the upkeep of the property from the will and stated that all of the beneficiaries were in agreement regarding the remodeling of the residence. The Court asked about the LLC that manages the property and asked if the trustee or anyone receive payment. Mr. Bret stated that the LLC is an operational entity owned by the trust, that there is no compensation to Mr. Sullivan, that there is a cost for the groundskeeper, that the LLC was formed to create limited liability, that they estimate about 6000 guests attending weddings per year and about 1500 personal guests per year, that it handles the winery across the street, that it handle leases, that at the end of 2015 the winery leased property was re-leased to generate $150,000.00 per year, that the trustee manages the LLC and that there is professional staff for the LLC. The Court stated that the other issue is the amount of compensation for Mr. Sullivan, having only spent 1200 hours on the administration. Mr. Bret stated that there have been no objections to the amounts, that previously Mr. Kolb waived his 50% fee as co-trustee, that now that Mr. Sullivan is the sole trustee he is requesting the full amount, that he maintains an office in San Francisco and that he is now keeping time sheets. The Court stated that the rules provide for different rates for professional and non professional trustees. Mr. Bret stated that Mr. Sullivan's experience and knowledge of the winery business far exceeds that of a professional trustee. Mr. Bret addressed the issue regarding receipts being detailed but expenditures grouped by category stating that the Probate Code allows for the LLC to do a separate accounting, that in the future the accountings will be broken down in detail, that he has a breakdown that can be filed and that he brought a revised proposed order as requested in the tentative ruling. The Court took the Thirty Second Account and Report of Trustee and Request for Approval of Trustee Fees and Attorney's Fees and Costs under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
